key_insight: Acoustoelastic Imaging (AEI) reconstructs internal stress fields directly from shear-wave propagation, turning stress mapping into an inverse wave problem rather than a constitutive-model-based inference.
engineering_relevance: This enables non-destructive, high-resolution stress imaging in heterogeneous solids and soft tissues, with potential impact on structural health monitoring and biomedical elastography.
method: Uses the acoustoelastic effect: pre-stress alters local shear-wave dynamics.
Performs full shear waveform inversion with physics-informed learning.
Recovers wave-equation coefficients from full-field measurements to estimate stress magnitude and principal directions without explicit material calibration.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical simulations and ultrasound shear wave elastography experiments demonstrating sub-wavelength resolution below 0.28λ and accurate reconstruction of nonuniform stress fields.
watch_item: Performance may depend on measurement quality, wave-field coverage, and the validity of the acoustoelastic assumptions in strongly heterogeneous or highly nonlinear materials.
paper_title: Direct stress imaging from shear wave propagation
